text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. Think of it like cleaning and reorganising a messy room: everything remains in the same place functionally, but it becomes easier to navigate. The main goal is to enhance readability, making the code simpler for developers to understand and modify. Refactoring also helps reduce technical debt—the long-term cost of taking shortcuts or writing messy code. By regularly cleaning up code, teams can avoid future problems and work more efficiently. Ultimately, refactoring is not about adding new features; it is about keeping the codebase healthy and manageable over time.
